title: Canarium baileyanum
intro: Canarium baileyanum, also known as the Queensland walnut, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Australia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that may aid in the management of various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Canarium baileyanum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
taxonomy: Canarium baileyanum is classified as a species of tree in the family Burseraceae, commonly known as the frankincense family. It is a member of the genus Canarium, which includes various species of trees and shrubs native to tropical regions. The Burseraceae family consists of approximately 170 species of trees and shrubs, primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions of the world. These plants are often characterized by their aromatic resin and fragrant wood. Canarium baileyanum is native to the Pacific island of New Caledonia.
uses: Canarium baileyanum has been reported to have various medicinal uses in traditional medicine. Its bark, leaves, and seeds are used to treat different health conditions. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating wounds and infections. In addition, it is also used to treat fever, cough, and other respiratory issues. However, more research is needed to fully understand and confirm its medicinal properties.
properties: Canarium baileyanum has been reported to possess various therapeutic properties, including antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. The plant's extracts have been shown to exhibit inhibitory effects on certain microorganisms, suggesting its potential as an antimicrobial agent. In addition, its antioxidant properties may help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. The plant's bioactive compounds may also possess anti-inflammatory and anticancer properties, although further research is needed to confirm these effects. Further studies are required to fully understand the therapeutic potential of Canarium baileyanum.

These compounds may have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The plant also contains alkaloids and other bioactive molecules that have been studied for their potential health benefits.
